Wisconsin Department of Public Instruction (DPI) announced RHTP-funded rural CTE healthcare workforce grant, opening Spring 2026. Up to $41,000 annually per school for 213 eligible rural LEAs ($5M over 5 years from DHS RHTP allocation). Grants support healthcare and human services career pathways, CTE courses, dual-credit, work-based learning, credentials, and Wisconsin HOSA chapters. Upcoming events: 2026 Wisconsin EMS Operations Management & Leadership Virtual Workshop (May 6, 2026); 2026 Annual Wisconsin Rural Health Conference (June 8-10, 2026, Elkhart Lake, WI).

notes: Wyoming received $205.0M for FY2026 ($5M more than requested, second-largest per-capita award behind Alaska). HB 122 SIGNED INTO LAW by Governor Gordon on March 6, 2026. The bill establishes the WY RHTP, creates advisory committee (9 voting members appointed by governor), perpetuity fund (originally proposed), and expenditure account. CMS APPROVAL RECEIVED (May 2026): Wyoming submitted an updated proposal to CMS in early May 2026; CMS approved the Year 1 plan in mid-May 2026 (announced ~May 19-22, 2026 by Gov. Gordon and Sen. Barrasso). The perpetuity fund was NOT approved - CMS determined states cannot implement trust funds or endowments. Wyoming now has until Oct 31, 2026 to obligate the first year of funding and one year to spend those funds. Four funding categories: (1) Access to basic emergency care (critical access hospitals and EMS); (2) Rural workforce supply (nursing, behavioral health, EMT education); (3) Health technology transformation (competitive applications for provider partnerships); (4) 'Make Wyoming Healthy Again' (primary care expansion). Per WDH timeline: May-June 2026 - state develops applications and requests for proposals; July 2026 - applications/procurement announcements released with 30-60 day submission period; October 2026 - deadline to obligate Year 1 funds; October 2027 - final deadline to fully spend Year 1 funds. Informational webinars and Q&A sessions to precede postings. Monitor health.wyo.gov RHTP page.
